cves.description: Windows SmartScreen security feature bypass vulnerability allowing attackers to bypass SmartScreen protection.
cves.fix_description: Apply the February 2024 Patch Tuesday update.

cves.description: Internet Shortcut Files security feature bypass vulnerability allowing attackers to bypass Mark of the Web warnings.
cves.fix_description: Apply the February 2024 Patch Tuesday update.

cves.description: Windows Kernel elevation of privilege vulnerability allowing local attackers to gain SYSTEM privileges.
cves.fix_description: Apply the June 2024 Patch Tuesday update.

cves.description: Windows Common Log File System Driver elevation of privilege vulnerability allowing local attackers to gain SYSTEM privileges.
cves.fix_description: Apply the December 2024 Patch Tuesday update.

cves.description: Windows OLE remote code execution vulnerability. Attackers can exploit this via a specially crafted email viewed in Microsoft Outlook.
cves.fix_description: Apply the January 2025 Patch Tuesday update. Read emails in plain text as a workaround.

cves.description: Windows Storage elevation of privilege vulnerability allowing local attackers to delete targeted files on a system leading to denial of service.
cves.fix_description: Apply the February 2025 Patch Tuesday update.

cves.description: Under specific conditions, a malicious webpage may trigger autofill population after two consecutive taps, potentially without clear or intentional user consent. This could result in disclosure of stored autofill data such as addresses, email, or phone number metadata.
cves.fix_description: Apply the latest security updates from the vendor.

cves.description: Microsoft Edge (Chromium-based) for Android Spoofing Vulnerability
cves.fix_description: Apply the latest security updates from the vendor.

cves.description: Improper access control in Windows Deployment Services allows an unauthorized attacker to execute code over an adjacent network.
cves.affected_software: Microsoft Windows Server 2008 R2 Service Pack 1, Microsoft Windows Server 2008 R2 Service Pack 1 (Server Core installation), Microsoft Windows Server 2008 Service Pack 2, Microsoft Windows Server 2008 Service Pack 2 (Server Core installation), Microsoft Windows Server 2012, Microsoft Windows Server 2012 (Server Core installation), Microsoft Windows Server 2012 R2, Microsoft Windows Server 2012 R2 (Server Core installation), Microsoft Windows Server 2016, Microsoft Windows Server 2016 (Server Core installation), Microsoft Windows Server 2019, Microsoft Windows Server 2019 (Server Core installation), Microsoft Windows Server 2022, Microsoft Windows Server 2022, 23H2 Edition (Server Core installation), Microsoft Windows Server 2025, Microsoft Windows Server 2025 (Server Core installation)
cves.fix_description: Apply the latest security updates from the vendor.

cves.description: User interface (ui) misrepresentation of critical information in Microsoft Edge for Android allows an unauthorized attacker to perform spoofing over a network.
cves.fix_description: Apply the latest security updates from the vendor.

cves.description: Improper Certificate Validation vulnerability in Thales SafeNet Agent for Windows Logon on Windows allows Signature Spoofing by Improper Validation.This issue affects SafeNet Agent for Windows Logon: 4.0.0, 4.1.1, 4.1.2.
cves.affected_software: Thales SafeNet Agent for Windows Logon
cves.fix_description: Apply the latest security updates from the vendor.

cves.description: Authentication Bypass Using an Alternate Path or Channel vulnerability in Drupal Microsoft Entra ID SSO Login allows Privilege Escalation.This issue affects Microsoft Entra ID SSO Login: from 0.0.0 before 1.0.4.
cves.affected_software: Drupal Microsoft Entra ID SSO Login
cves.fix_description: Apply the latest security updates from the vendor.

cves.description: A medium-severity vulnerability has been identified in BeyondTrust Privilege Management for Windows versions <=25.7. Under certain conditions, a local authenticated user with elevated privileges may be able to bypass the productâ€™s anti-tamper protections, which could allow access to protected application components and the ability to modify product configuration.
cves.fix_description: Apply the latest security updates from the vendor.

cves.description: Windows TCP/IP remote code execution vulnerability in the IPv6 stack allowing unauthenticated attackers to send specially crafted IPv6 packets to execute arbitrary code.
cves.fix_description: Apply the August 2024 Patch Tuesday security update. Disable IPv6 as a temporary mitigation if patching is not immediately possible.

cves.description: Windows Scripting Engine memory corruption vulnerability allowing remote code execution when a user visits a specially crafted webpage using Edge in Internet Explorer Mode.
cves.fix_description: Apply the August 2024 security update from Microsoft.

cves.description: Windows Ancillary Function Driver for WinSock elevation of privilege vulnerability exploited in the wild by the Lazarus Group to gain SYSTEM-level privileges.
cves.fix_description: Apply the August 2024 security update.

cves.description: Windows Kernel elevation of privilege vulnerability exploited in the wild allowing an attacker to gain SYSTEM privileges through a race condition.
cves.fix_description: Apply the August 2024 Patch Tuesday update.

cves.description: Microsoft Windows Update remote code execution vulnerability that re-introduced previously mitigated vulnerabilities on certain Windows 10 systems through a servicing stack rollback.
cves.fix_description: Install the September 2024 Servicing Stack Update (KB5043936) and then the September 2024 security update.

cves.description: Windows Management Console (MMC) remote code execution vulnerability allowing attackers to execute arbitrary code via malicious Microsoft Saved Console (.msc) files.
cves.fix_description: Apply the October 2024 Patch Tuesday update. Microsoft now prevents untrusted MSC files from being opened.

cves.description: Windows MSHTML Platform spoofing vulnerability exploited in the wild, allowing attackers to trick users through crafted content rendered via the MSHTML engine.
cves.fix_description: Apply the October 2024 security update.

cves.description: Windows Task Scheduler elevation of privilege vulnerability allowing an authenticated attacker to elevate from a low-privilege AppContainer to Medium Integrity Level by abusing Task Scheduler RPC functions.
cves.fix_description: Apply the November 2024 Patch Tuesday update.

cves.description: Windows Wi-Fi Driver remote code execution vulnerability allowing an unauthenticated attacker within Wi-Fi range to send specially crafted packets and execute code without user interaction.
cves.fix_description: Apply the June 2024 Patch Tuesday security update.

cves.description: Windows Kernel elevation of privilege vulnerability in the appid.sys AppLocker driver exploited by the Lazarus Group to gain kernel-level read/write access and disable security software.
cves.fix_description: Apply the February 2024 Patch Tuesday update.

cves.description: Windows Error Reporting Service elevation of privilege vulnerability allowing an attacker to gain SYSTEM privileges. Exploited in the wild by ransomware operators.
cves.fix_description: Apply the March 2024 security update.

cves.description: Microsoft Message Queuing (MSMQ) remote code execution vulnerability allowing unauthenticated attackers to execute arbitrary code by sending specially crafted MSMQ packets.
cves.fix_description: Apply the June 2024 Patch Tuesday update. Disable MSMQ service if not required.

cves.description: Windows Remote Desktop Services remote code execution vulnerability allowing an unauthenticated attacker to execute arbitrary code on the target RDP server.
cves.fix_description: Apply the January 2025 Patch Tuesday update. Restrict RDP access via firewall rules.

cves.description: Windows NTLM V1 elevation of privilege vulnerability allowing attackers to escalate privileges by exploiting weaknesses in NTLMv1 authentication.
cves.fix_description: Apply the January 2025 security update. Disable NTLMv1 and enforce NTLMv2 or Kerberos.

cves.description: Microsoft Bing remote code execution vulnerability due to missing authentication allowing an unauthenticated attacker to execute code over a network.
cves.fix_description: No user action required. Microsoft has addressed this vulnerability on the server side.

cves.description: Windows Ancillary Function Driver for WinSock elevation of privilege vulnerability exploited in the wild allowing an attacker to gain SYSTEM privileges.
cves.fix_description: Apply the February 2025 Patch Tuesday update.

cves.description: Windows Lightweight Directory Access Protocol (LDAP) remote code execution vulnerability allowing an unauthenticated attacker to execute code by sending crafted LDAP requests.
cves.fix_description: Apply the February 2025 security update.

cves.description: Windows Fast FAT File System Driver remote code execution vulnerability allowing code execution via a specially crafted VHD file. Exploited in the wild.
cves.fix_description: Apply the March 2025 Patch Tuesday update.

cves.description: Windows Win32 Kernel Subsystem elevation of privilege vulnerability exploited in the wild as a zero-day, allowing a local attacker to gain SYSTEM privileges via a race condition.
cves.fix_description: Apply the March 2025 Patch Tuesday update.

cves.description: Windows NTFS information disclosure vulnerability exploited in the wild allowing an attacker with physical access to read portions of heap memory via a malicious USB device.
cves.fix_description: Apply the March 2025 security update.

cves.description: Windows NTFS out-of-bounds read information disclosure vulnerability exploited in the wild allowing an attacker to read sensitive kernel memory.
cves.fix_description: Apply the March 2025 Patch Tuesday update.

cves.description: Windows NTFS remote code execution vulnerability exploited in the wild via specially crafted VHD files that trigger a heap-based buffer overflow.
cves.fix_description: Apply the March 2025 security update. Exercise caution when mounting VHD files from untrusted sources.

cves.description: Microsoft Management Console (MMC) security feature bypass vulnerability exploited in the wild allowing attackers to bypass file reputation protections via MSC files.
cves.fix_description: Apply the March 2025 Patch Tuesday update.

cves.description: Some web servers under Microsoft Windows allow remote attackers to bypass access restrictions for files with long file names.
cves.fix_description: Apply the latest security updates from Microsoft.

cves.description: The WINS server in Microsoft Windows NT 4.0 before SP4 allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (process termination) via invalid UDP frames to port 137 (NETBIOS Name Service), as demonstrated via a flood of random packets.
cves.fix_description: Apply the latest security updates from Microsoft.

cves.description: TCP/IP implementation in Microsoft Windows 95, Windows NT 4.0, and possibly others, allows remote attackers to reset connections by forcing a reset (RST) via a PSH ACK or other means, obtaining the target's last sequence number from the resulting packet, then spoofing a reset to the target.
cves.fix_description: Apply the latest security updates from Microsoft.

cves.description: Microsoft Personal Web Server and FrontPage Personal Web Server in some Windows systems allows a remote attacker to read files on the server by using a nonstandard URL.
cves.fix_description: Apply the latest security updates from Microsoft.

cves.description: Buffer overflow in Microsoft Telnet client in Windows 95 and Windows 98 via a malformed Telnet argument.
cves.fix_description: Apply the latest security updates from Microsoft.

cves.description: The rdisk utility in Microsoft Terminal Server Edition and Windows NT 4.0 stores registry hive information in a temporary file with permissions that allow local users to read it, aka the "RDISK Registry Enumeration File" vulnerability.
cves.fix_description: Apply the latest security updates from Microsoft.

cves.description: Microsoft email clients in Outlook, Exchange, and Windows Messaging automatically respond to Read Receipt and Delivery Receipt tags, which could allow an attacker to flood a mail system with responses by forging a Read Receipt request that is redirected to a large distribution list.
cves.fix_description: Apply the latest security updates from Microsoft.

cves.description: Microsoft Windows 9x operating systems allow an attacker to cause a denial of service via a pathname that includes file device names, aka the "DOS Device in Path Name" vulnerability.
cves.fix_description: Apply the latest security updates from Microsoft.

cves.description: Microsoft Windows Media License Manager allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service by sending a malformed request that causes the manager to halt, aka the "Malformed Media License Request" Vulnerability.
cves.fix_description: Apply the latest security updates from Microsoft.

cves.description: The default configuration for the domain name resolver for Microsoft Windows 98, NT 4.0, 2000, and XP sets the QueryIpMatching parameter to 0, which causes Windows to accept DNS updates from hosts that it did not query, which allows remote attackers to poison the DNS cache.
cves.fix_description: Apply the latest security updates from Microsoft.

cves.description: Buffer overflow in Microsoft command processor (CMD.EXE) for Windows NT and Windows 2000 allows a local user to cause a denial of service via a long environment variable, aka the "Malformed Environment Variable" vulnerability.
cves.fix_description: Apply the latest security updates from Microsoft.

cves.description: Microsoft Windows Media Encoder allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service via a malformed request, aka the "Malformed Windows Media Encoder Request" vulnerability.
cves.fix_description: Apply the latest security updates from Microsoft.

cves.description: Microsoft Outlook Express allows remote attackers to monitor a user's email by creating a persistent browser link to the Outlook Express windows, aka the "Persistent Mail-Browser Link" vulnerability.
cves.fix_description: Apply the latest security updates from Microsoft.

cves.description: Interactions between the CIFS Browser Protocol and NetBIOS as implemented in Microsoft Windows 95, 98, NT, and 2000 allow remote attackers to modify dynamic NetBIOS name cache entries via a spoofed Browse Frame Request in a unicast or UDP broadcast datagram.
cves.fix_description: Apply the latest security updates from Microsoft.

cves.description: The IPX protocol implementation in Microsoft Windows 95 and 98 allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service by sending a ping packet with a source IP address that is a broadcast address, aka the "Malformed IPX Ping Packet" vulnerability.
cves.fix_description: Apply the latest security updates from Microsoft.

cves.description: Microsoft Windows 2000 allows local users to cause a denial of service by corrupting the local security policy via malformed RPC traffic, aka the "Local Security Policy Corruption" vulnerability.
cves.fix_description: Apply the latest security updates from Microsoft.

cves.description: The web-based folder display capability in Microsoft Internet Explorer 5.5 on Windows 98 allows local users to insert Trojan horse programs by modifying the Folder.htt file and using the InvokeVerb method in the ShellDefView ActiveX control to specify a default execute option for the first file that is listed in the folder.
cves.fix_description: Apply the latest security updates from Microsoft.

cves.description: Race condition in Microsoft Windows Media server allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service in the Windows Media Unicast Service via a malformed request, aka the "Unicast Service Race Condition" vulnerability.
cves.fix_description: Apply the latest security updates from Microsoft.

cves.description: Vulnerability in Microsoft Windows NT 4.0 allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service in IIS by sending it a series of malformed requests which cause INETINFO.EXE to fail, aka the "Invalid URL" vulnerability.
cves.fix_description: Apply the latest security updates from Microsoft.

cves.description: Microsoft Windows 2000 before Service Pack 2 (SP2), when running in a non-Windows 2000 domain and using NTLM authentication, and when credentials of an account are locally cached, allows local users to bypass account lockout policies and make an unlimited number of login attempts, aka the "Domain Account Lockout" vulnerability.
cves.fix_description: Apply the latest security updates from Microsoft.

cves.description: Microsoft Windows Media Player 7 allows attackers to cause a denial of service in RTF-enabled email clients via an embedded OCX control that is not closed properly, aka the "OCX Attachment" vulnerability.
cves.fix_description: Apply the latest security updates from Microsoft.

cves.description: The CiWebHitsFile component in Microsoft Indexing Services for Windows 2000 allows remote attackers to conduct a cross site scripting (CSS) attack via a CiRestriction parameter in a .htw request, aka the "Indexing Services Cross Site Scripting" vulnerability.
cves.fix_description: Apply the latest security updates from Microsoft.

cves.description: Microsoft Windows Media Player 7 executes scripts in custom skin (.WMS) files, which could allow remote attackers to gain privileges via a skin that contains a malicious script, aka the ".WMS Script Execution" vulnerability.
cves.fix_description: Apply the latest security updates from Microsoft.

cves.description: Buffer overflow in Microsoft Windows Media Player allows remote attackers to execute arbitrary commands via a malformed Active Stream Redirector (.ASX) file, aka the ".ASX Buffer Overrun" vulnerability.
cves.fix_description: Apply the latest security updates from Microsoft.

cves.description: Web Extender Client (WEC) in Microsoft Office 2000, Windows 2000, and Windows Me does not properly process Internet Explorer security settings for NTLM authentication, which allows attackers to obtain NTLM credentials and possibly obtain the password, aka the "Web Client NTLM Authentication" vulnerability.
cves.fix_description: Apply the latest security updates from Microsoft.

cves.description: The default permissions for the MTS Package Administration registry key in Windows NT 4.0 allows local users to install or modify arbitrary Microsoft Transaction Server (MTS) packages and gain privileges, aka one of the "Registry Permissions" vulnerabilities.
cves.fix_description: Apply the latest security updates from Microsoft.

cves.description: Buffer overflow in Microsoft FrontPage Server Extensions (PWS) 3.0.2.926 on Windows 95, and possibly other versions, allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service via a long URL.
cves.fix_description: Apply the latest security updates from Microsoft.

cves.description: Microsoft Windows 2000 Encrypted File System does not properly destroy backups of files that are encrypted, which allows a local attacker to recover the text of encrypted files.
cves.fix_description: Apply the latest security updates from Microsoft.

cves.description: Buffer overflows in Microsoft Windows Media Player 7 and earlier allow remote attackers to execute arbitrary commands via (1) a long version tag in an .ASX file, or (2) a long banner tag, a variant of the ".ASX Buffer Overrun" vulnerability as discussed in MS:MS00-090.
cves.fix_description: Apply the latest security updates from Microsoft.

cves.description: Microsoft Index Server 2.0 in Windows NT 4.0, and Indexing Service in Windows 2000, allows remote attackers to read server-side include files via a malformed search request, aka a new variant of the "Malformed Hit-Highlighting" vulnerability.
cves.fix_description: Apply the latest security updates from Microsoft.

cves.description: Microsoft Windows 2000 telnet service allows attackers to prevent idle Telnet sessions from timing out, causing a denial of service by creating a large number of idle sessions.
cves.fix_description: Apply the latest security updates from Microsoft.

cves.description: Handle leak in Microsoft Windows 2000 telnet service allows attackers to cause a denial of service by starting a large number of sessions and terminating them.
cves.fix_description: Apply the latest security updates from Microsoft.

cves.description: Information disclosure vulnerability in Microsoft Windows 2000 telnet service allows remote attackers to determine the existence of user accounts such as Guest, or log in to the server without specifying the domain name, via a malformed userid.
cves.fix_description: Apply the latest security updates from Microsoft.

cves.description: Microsoft Windows 2000 telnet service allows attackers to cause a denial of service (crash) via a long logon command that contains a backspace.
cves.fix_description: Apply the latest security updates from Microsoft.

cves.description: Microsoft Windows 2000 telnet service creates named pipes with predictable names and does not properly verify them, which allows local users to execute arbitrary commands by creating a named pipe with the predictable name and associating a malicious program with it, the first of two variants of this vulnerability.
cves.fix_description: Apply the latest security updates from Microsoft.

cves.description: Microsoft Windows 2000 telnet service creates named pipes with predictable names and does not properly verify them, which allows local users to execute arbitrary commands by creating a named pipe with the predictable name and associating a malicious program with it, the second of two variants of this vulnerability.
cves.fix_description: Apply the latest security updates from Microsoft.

cves.description: Microsoft Windows 2000 telnet service allows a local user to make a certain system call that allows the user to terminate a Telnet session and cause a denial of service.
cves.fix_description: Apply the latest security updates from Microsoft.

cves.description: The Microsoft Windows network stack allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (CPU consumption) via a flood of malformed ARP request packets with random source IP and MAC addresses, as demonstrated by ARPNuke.
cves.fix_description: Apply the latest security updates from Microsoft.

cves.description: Vulnerability in authentication process for SMTP service in Microsoft Windows 2000 allows remote attackers to use incorrect credentials to gain privileges and conduct activities such as mail relaying.
cves.fix_description: Apply the latest security updates from Microsoft.

cves.description: Vulnerabilities in RPC servers in (1) Microsoft Exchange Server 2000 and earlier, (2) Microsoft SQL Server 2000 and earlier, (3) Windows NT 4.0, and (4) Windows 2000 allow remote attackers to cause a denial of service via malformed inputs.
cves.fix_description: Apply the latest security updates from Microsoft.

cves.description: Buffer overflow in Microsoft Windows Media Player 7.1 and earlier allows remote attackers to execute arbitrary commands via a malformed Windows Media Station (.NSC) file.
cves.fix_description: Apply the latest security updates from Microsoft.

cves.description: Buffer overflow in helpctr.exe program in Microsoft Help Center for Windows XP allows remote attackers to execute arbitrary code via a long hcp: URL.
cves.fix_description: Apply the latest security updates from Microsoft.

cves.description: Buffer overflow in Microsoft Windows Media Player 6.4 allows remote attackers to execute arbitrary code via a malformed Advanced Streaming Format (ASF) file.
cves.fix_description: Apply the latest security updates from Microsoft.

cves.description: Microsoft Windows XP allows local users to bypass a locked screen and run certain programs that are associated with Hot Keys.
cves.fix_description: Apply the latest security updates from Microsoft.

cves.description: In Microsoft Windows NT and Windows 2000, a trusting domain that receives authorization information from a trusted domain does not verify that the trusted domain is authoritative for all listed SIDs, which allows remote attackers to gain Domain Administrator privileges on the trusting domain by injecting SIDs from untrusted domains into the authorization data that comes from from the trusted domain.
cves.fix_description: Apply the latest security updates from Microsoft.

cves.description: SMTP service in (1) Microsoft Windows 2000 and (2) Internet Mail Connector (IMC) in Exchange Server 5.5 does not properly handle responses to NTLM authentication, which allows remote attackers to perform mail relaying via an SMTP AUTH command using null session credentials.
cves.fix_description: Apply the latest security updates from Microsoft.

cves.description: SMTP service in Microsoft Windows 2000, Windows XP Professional, and Exchange 2000 allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service via a command with a malformed data transfer (BDAT) request.
cves.fix_description: Apply the latest security updates from Microsoft.

cves.description: Microsoft Internet Explorer 5.5 on Windows 98 allows remote web pages to cause a denial of service (hang) via extremely long values for form fields such as INPUT and TEXTAREA, which can be automatically filled via Javascript.
cves.fix_description: Apply the latest security updates from Microsoft.

cves.description: Buffer overflow in Multiple UNC Provider (MUP) in Microsoft Windows operating systems allows local users to cause a denial of service or possibly gain SYSTEM privileges via a long UNC request.
cves.fix_description: Apply the latest security updates from Microsoft.

cves.description: The MSDTC (Microsoft Distributed Transaction Service Coordinator) for Microsoft Windows 2000, Microsoft IIS 5.0 and SQL Server 6.5 through SQL 2000 0.0 allows remote attackers to cause a denial of service (crash or hang) via malformed (random) input.
cves.fix_description: Apply the latest security updates from Microsoft.
